Off Calendar Mar-09-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
RULING:
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. CROSS DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is taken UNDER SUBMISSION. (301/JLW) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Dec-08-2004 Continued To Dec-22-04 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Sarah Ellen Owsowitz, Deputy City Attorney (415)554-4618, for cross defendant and moving party: City and county of San Francisco
Jan Vaughn Mock, Nossaman Guthner Knox Ellion LLP (415)398-3600, for Plaintiff and opposing party: Nextmedia Outdood, Inc.

RULING:
A R G U E D. Upon conclusion of Counsel's argument, the Court states its Tentative Ruling: CROSS DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is GRANTED to the extent that Plaintiff is seeking relief from the Board's final decision to uphold revocation of the permit; Plaintiff may amend to delete causes that have been statutorily barred. Cross Defendant City and County of San Francisco's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is CONTINUED TO 12/22/04 in Dept. 301 to be taken under submisison at that time. The Court directs counsel submit a joint form of order no later than 12/22/04. (301/JLW/EH) Reporter: Christina T. Paxton, CSR #1558

Motion For Preliminary Injunction; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Oct-28-2004 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jan Vaughn Mock, Esq., Nossman Guthner Knox Elliott LLP (415) 398-3600 for Petitioner and Moving Party Nextmedia Outdoor, Inc.
Sarah Ellen Owsowitz, Deputy City Attorney (415) 554-4618 for Respondent and Opposing Party City and County of San Francisco
RULING:
A R G U E D; the Court adopted its Tentative Ruling: PETITIONER NEXTMEDIA OUTDOOR, INC'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction: DENIED; Petitioner has failed to demonstrate irreparable harm (all damages involved are monetary); adequate remedy at law exists. Order signed. (301/JLW/EH) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558
